refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on minimizing energy per ton moved aligns with the acceptance criteria and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and verify energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operation to reduce energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to adjust operations for energy efficiency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Integrate energy metering and telemetry for real-time energy monitoring.
DP2: Implement adaptive vehicle control algorithms to optimize energy use.
DP3: Develop operator feedback and tasking interface to guide efficient operation.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 + DP2 + DP3 combined to address monitoring, optimization, and feedback for energy efficiency.

potential_sources: Telemetry data shared between energy metering and vehicle control modules.
Operator feedback loop coupling tasking and vehicle control.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and manageable with clear interface definitions and modular separation.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate and timely telemetry data is available.
Risk of suboptimal control if feedback latency is high.
Risk of complexity in integrating control algorithms with vehicle hardware.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure clear interface definitions and modular separation to manage coupling and integration complexity.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical vehicle systems including energy metering hardware and adaptive control software.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends energy consumption and operational data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational commands and efficiency targets from NGHE Solution and Teleoperation.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of energy metering and control logic within Vehicle; low diffusion as interfaces are well-defined.
Contains system-level monitoring and KPI aggregation; moderate diffusion due to cross-module data sharing.
Limited containment; mainly receives tasking and provides operator input.
Minimal containment related to energy efficiency; mostly safety alerts.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency control and monitoring are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize diffusion and maintain tight control over hardware and software integration.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in integrating adaptive control algorithms within Vehicle hardware constraints.
Risk of insufficient coordination with NGHE Solution for system-level KPI aggregation.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and ensure NGHE Solution aggregates and verifies KPIs without duplicating control logic.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well-defined and measurable; selected DPs logically address the FRs with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Moderate diffusion between Vehicle and NGHE Solution for telemetry and KPI data sharing.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment within Vehicle for control and metering is strong, minimizing diffusion risks.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring energy consumption and tons moved, and validating telemetry data from Vehicle module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of energy efficiency functions to Vehicle with system-level KPI aggregation in NGHE Solution.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface specifications between Vehicle and NGHE Solution for telemetry and KPI data.
Plan integration testing to validate adaptive control algorithms within Vehicle constraints.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, interface definitions and integration risks require refinement before proceeding to next level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operation to reduce energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to adjust operations for energy efficiency.

revise.why: To refine interface contracts and reduce diffusion risks between Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules.

assumptions: Telemetry data is accurate and timely.
Vehicle hardware supports adaptive control algorithms.
open_risks: Integration complexity of control algorithms within Vehicle.
Potential latency in feedback loops affecting energy optimization.
evidence_needed: Performance benchmarks of adaptive control algorithms on Vehicle hardware.
